stages.title: Foundations & Prerequisites
stages.summary: Data, storage, databases, SQL and systems concepts NoSQL builds on.

nodes.title: Data & Storage Fundamentals
nodes.description: How data is represented, stored and structured on disk and in memory.

nodes.title: Databases Overview
nodes.description: What databases are, how a DBMS is built and the main workloads.

nodes.title: Relational Databases & SQL Recap
nodes.description: The relational model, SQL and ACID — the baseline NoSQL departs from.

nodes.title: OS, Networking & Concurrency Basics
nodes.description: Operating-system, networking and concurrency concepts databases rely on.

stages.title: NoSQL Fundamentals
stages.summary: What NoSQL is, how it differs from SQL, the database families and core concepts.

nodes.title: What is NoSQL
nodes.description: Definition, motivations and core characteristics of NoSQL databases.

nodes.title: NoSQL vs SQL
nodes.description: How NoSQL differs from relational databases and how to choose.

nodes.title: Types of NoSQL Databases
nodes.description: The main NoSQL families and what each is good for.

nodes.title: Core NoSQL Concepts
nodes.description: Foundational concepts shared across NoSQL systems.

stages.title: Distributed Systems Theory
stages.summary: CAP/PACELC, consistency, replication, partitioning, consensus and transactions.

nodes.title: Distributed Systems Basics
nodes.description: Core ideas, models and fallacies behind distributed databases.

nodes.title: CAP, PACELC & Consistency Theory
nodes.description: The theoretical limits that shape NoSQL design decisions.

nodes.title: Replication
nodes.description: Copying data across nodes for availability and read scaling.

nodes.title: Partitioning & Sharding
nodes.description: Splitting data across nodes to scale storage and throughput.

nodes.title: Consensus & Coordination
nodes.description: Agreement protocols and coordination services for distributed state.

nodes.title: Distributed Transactions
nodes.description: Atomicity and isolation across multiple nodes and partitions.

stages.title: Data Modeling for NoSQL
stages.summary: Query-driven modeling, modeling per database type, design patterns and governance.

nodes.title: NoSQL Data Modeling Principles
nodes.description: The mindset shift from relational to query-driven NoSQL modeling.

nodes.title: Modeling by Database Type
nodes.description: How modeling differs across each NoSQL family.

nodes.title: Data Modeling Patterns
nodes.description: Reusable schema design patterns for NoSQL.

nodes.title: Schema & Data Governance
nodes.description: Managing schema evolution, validation and data quality in NoSQL.

stages.title: Key-Value Stores
stages.summary: The key-value model and its main databases: Redis, DynamoDB, Memcached and more.

nodes.title: Key-Value Store Fundamentals
nodes.description: The simplest NoSQL model: keys mapped to opaque values.

nodes.title: Redis
nodes.description: The most popular in-memory data store (Redis 8 / Open Source).

nodes.title: Amazon DynamoDB
nodes.description: Fully-managed, serverless key-value and document database on AWS.

nodes.title: Memcached
nodes.description: Simple, high-performance distributed memory caching system.

nodes.title: Other Key-Value Stores
nodes.description: Specialized and embedded key-value engines beyond Redis & DynamoDB.

stages.title: Document Databases
stages.summary: The document model and its databases: MongoDB, Couchbase, CouchDB, Firestore and more.

nodes.title: Document Database Fundamentals
nodes.description: Storing semi-structured documents (JSON/BSON) as first-class records.

nodes.title: MongoDB
nodes.description: The leading document database (MongoDB 8.x).

nodes.title: Couchbase
nodes.description: Distributed document database with memory-first architecture and SQL++.

nodes.title: Apache CouchDB
nodes.description: HTTP/JSON document database built for offline-first sync.

nodes.title: Cloud Firestore
nodes.description: Google’s serverless document database with realtime sync.

nodes.title: Other Document Databases
nodes.description: Additional document stores and MongoDB-compatible engines.

stages.title: Wide-Column Stores
stages.summary: Column-family databases: Cassandra, ScyllaDB, HBase and Bigtable.

nodes.title: Wide-Column Store Fundamentals
nodes.description: Column-family databases optimized for huge write-heavy workloads.

nodes.title: Apache Cassandra
nodes.description: Masterless, highly-available wide-column database (Cassandra 5.x).

nodes.title: ScyllaDB
nodes.description: C++ rewrite of Cassandra with shard-per-core architecture.

nodes.title: Apache HBase
nodes.description: Hadoop-based wide-column store modeled on Google Bigtable.

nodes.title: Google Cloud Bigtable
nodes.description: Google’s fully-managed wide-column database that inspired the family.

nodes.title: Other Wide-Column / Compatible
nodes.description: Managed and compatible wide-column services.

stages.title: Graph Databases
stages.summary: The graph model, query languages and databases: Neo4j, Neptune and more.

nodes.title: Graph Database Fundamentals
nodes.description: Modeling and storing highly-connected data as nodes and relationships.

nodes.title: Graph Query Languages
nodes.description: Languages for querying and traversing graphs (Cypher, GQL, Gremlin, SPARQL).

nodes.title: Neo4j
nodes.description: The most popular native graph database.

nodes.title: Amazon Neptune
nodes.description: Fully-managed graph database supporting LPG and RDF.

nodes.title: Other Graph Databases
nodes.description: Distributed, multi-model and specialized graph databases.

nodes.title: Graph Algorithms & Analytics
nodes.description: Algorithms that extract insight from graph structure.

stages.title: Search Engines
stages.summary: Full-text search engines as NoSQL stores: Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, Solr.

nodes.title: Search Engine Fundamentals
nodes.description: How full-text search engines index and rank documents.

nodes.title: Elasticsearch
nodes.description: The leading distributed search & analytics engine (Elasticsearch 9.x).

nodes.title: OpenSearch
nodes.description: Open-source fork of Elasticsearch maintained by the OpenSearch Foundation.

nodes.title: Apache Solr
nodes.description: Mature Lucene-based enterprise search platform.

nodes.title: Other Search Engines
nodes.description: Modern, developer-friendly and large-scale search engines.

stages.title: Time-Series Databases
stages.summary: Databases for timestamped data: InfluxDB, TimescaleDB, Prometheus and more.

nodes.title: Time-Series Database Fundamentals
nodes.description: Databases optimized for timestamped, append-heavy data.

nodes.title: InfluxDB
nodes.description: Popular purpose-built time-series database (InfluxDB 3.x).

nodes.title: TimescaleDB
nodes.description: PostgreSQL extension turning Postgres into a time-series database.

nodes.title: Prometheus
nodes.description: Pull-based metrics & monitoring time-series database.

nodes.title: Other Time-Series Databases
nodes.description: High-performance and managed time-series databases.

stages.title: Vector Databases
stages.summary: Embedding storage and similarity search: Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus, Qdrant and more.

nodes.title: Vector Database Fundamentals
nodes.description: Storing and searching high-dimensional embeddings by similarity.

nodes.title: Pinecone
nodes.description: Fully-managed serverless vector database.

nodes.title: Weaviate
nodes.description: Open-source vector database with built-in modules and GraphQL.

nodes.title: Milvus
nodes.description: Open-source vector database built for billion-scale workloads.

nodes.title: Qdrant
nodes.description: High-performance open-source vector database written in Rust.

nodes.title: Other Vector Databases & Integrations
nodes.description: Embedded vector stores and vector features in existing databases.

nodes.title: Vector Search in Practice
nodes.description: Building real applications on top of vector databases.

stages.title: Multi-Model & Other NoSQL
stages.summary: Multi-model and NewSQL databases, object stores, event stores and specialized engines.

nodes.title: Multi-Model Databases
nodes.description: Databases that support several data models in one engine.

nodes.title: NewSQL & Distributed SQL
nodes.description: Systems combining SQL and ACID with NoSQL-style horizontal scaling.

nodes.title: Object & Blob Stores
nodes.description: Massively-scalable storage for unstructured objects and files.

nodes.title: Streaming & Event Stores
nodes.description: Log-structured systems that store data as immutable event streams.

nodes.title: Other Specialized Stores
nodes.description: Ledger, in-memory grid, RDF and embedded databases.

stages.title: Querying, Indexing & APIs
stages.summary: Query languages, indexing strategies, aggregation, APIs and transactions in practice.

nodes.title: NoSQL Query Languages
nodes.description: The diverse query languages and paradigms across NoSQL databases.

nodes.title: Indexing Strategies
nodes.description: How indexes work across NoSQL and how to design them.

nodes.title: Aggregation & Analytics
nodes.description: Aggregating, transforming and analyzing data in NoSQL.

nodes.title: Database APIs & Access
nodes.description: How applications connect to and access NoSQL databases.

nodes.title: Transactions & Consistency in Practice
nodes.description: Using transactions and consistency controls in real NoSQL apps.

stages.title: Performance, Scaling & Reliability
stages.summary: Performance tuning, caching, scaling, resilience and capacity planning.

nodes.title: Performance Fundamentals
nodes.description: Measuring and improving NoSQL read/write performance.

nodes.title: Caching Strategies
nodes.description: Using caches to reduce database load and latency.

nodes.title: Scaling Strategies
nodes.description: Growing capacity and throughput as load increases.

nodes.title: Reliability & Resilience
nodes.description: Keeping databases available and durable under failures.

nodes.title: Capacity Planning & Benchmarking
nodes.description: Sizing clusters and measuring database performance objectively.

stages.title: Security & Compliance
stages.summary: Authentication, access control, encryption, network security, threats and compliance.

nodes.title: Authentication
nodes.description: Verifying the identity of users and services accessing the database.

nodes.title: Authorization & Access Control
nodes.description: Controlling what authenticated principals can do.

nodes.title: Data Protection & Encryption
nodes.description: Protecting data at rest, in transit and in use.

nodes.title: Network Security
nodes.description: Securing the network perimeter around the database.

nodes.title: Security Threats & Auditing
nodes.description: Common attacks against NoSQL and how to detect them.

nodes.title: Compliance & Governance
nodes.description: Meeting regulatory and data-governance requirements.

stages.title: Operations, Monitoring & DevOps
stages.summary: Deployment, monitoring, backup/recovery, maintenance, automation and migration.

nodes.title: Deployment & Provisioning
nodes.description: Installing, configuring and deploying NoSQL databases.

nodes.title: Monitoring & Observability
nodes.description: Tracking the health and performance of databases in production.

nodes.title: Backup & Recovery
nodes.description: Protecting data with backups and tested recovery procedures.

nodes.title: Maintenance & Upgrades
nodes.description: Routine maintenance and safe version upgrades.

nodes.title: DevOps & Automation
nodes.description: Treating databases as code with automation and CI/CD.

nodes.title: Data Migration
nodes.description: Moving data between databases and platforms safely.

stages.title: Cloud & Managed NoSQL
stages.summary: Managed NoSQL across AWS, Azure, GCP and independent DBaaS, plus cloud cost.

nodes.title: Cloud Database Concepts
nodes.description: Managed, serverless and multi-cloud database fundamentals.

nodes.title: AWS NoSQL Services
nodes.description: Amazon’s managed NoSQL and data services.

nodes.title: Azure NoSQL Services
nodes.description: Microsoft Azure’s NoSQL offerings, led by Cosmos DB.

nodes.title: Google Cloud NoSQL Services
nodes.description: Google Cloud’s managed NoSQL and data services.

nodes.title: Independent DBaaS Providers
nodes.description: Vendor-managed cloud services across multiple clouds.

nodes.title: Cloud Cost & FinOps
nodes.description: Understanding and optimizing managed database costs.

stages.title: Integration & Ecosystem
stages.summary: App integration, CDC, streaming, ETL, polyglot persistence and AI/ML integration.

nodes.title: Application Integration
nodes.description: Connecting NoSQL databases cleanly into application architectures.

nodes.title: Change Data Capture (CDC)
nodes.description: Streaming database changes to downstream systems in real time.

nodes.title: Streaming & Event Pipelines
nodes.description: Integrating NoSQL with streaming platforms and processors.

nodes.title: Data Pipelines & ETL
nodes.description: Moving NoSQL data into analytics and warehousing systems.

nodes.title: Polyglot Persistence
nodes.description: Using multiple databases together in one system.

nodes.title: AI & ML Integration
nodes.description: Using NoSQL databases as part of AI and ML systems.

stages.title: Advanced Patterns
stages.summary: Event sourcing, CQRS, distributed transactions and advanced data/scaling patterns.

nodes.title: Event Sourcing
nodes.description: Persisting state as an immutable sequence of events.

nodes.title: CQRS
nodes.description: Command Query Responsibility Segregation.

nodes.title: Distributed Transaction Patterns
nodes.description: Maintaining consistency across services without distributed locks.

nodes.title: Consistency & Messaging Patterns
nodes.description: Patterns for idempotency, deduplication and delivery guarantees.

nodes.title: Advanced Data & Scaling Patterns
nodes.description: Patterns for global, multi-tenant and large-scale data systems.

stages.title: Architecture, Use Cases & Choosing
stages.summary: Choosing a database, reference architectures, use cases, anti-patterns and case studies.

nodes.title: Choosing the Right Database
nodes.description: A structured approach to selecting the right NoSQL database.

nodes.title: Reference Architectures
nodes.description: Proven architectural blueprints using NoSQL.

nodes.title: Industry Use Cases
nodes.description: How different industries apply NoSQL databases.

nodes.title: Anti-Patterns & Pitfalls
nodes.description: Common mistakes to avoid when using NoSQL.

nodes.title: Case Studies at Scale
nodes.description: Lessons from large-scale real-world NoSQL deployments.

stages.title: Frontier Trends & Career
stages.summary: Emerging trends, AI & databases, research frontiers, learning resources and careers.

nodes.description: Where NoSQL and data systems are heading as of 2026.

nodes.description: The deepening relationship between AI and data systems.

nodes.description: Open problems and academic directions in NoSQL and databases.

nodes.description: Where to keep learning about NoSQL and data systems.

nodes.description: Building a career around NoSQL and data engineering.

nodes.title: Enterprise NoSQL Strategy & Operating Model
nodes.description: Strategy, operating model and NoSQL center of excellence.

nodes.title: Discovery & Requirements Engineering
nodes.description: Use case discovery, business case and requirements engineering for NoSQL data systems.

nodes.description: Architecture decisions, design reviews and reference blueprints for NoSQL data systems.

nodes.description: Coding standards, peer review, definition of done and quality gates.

nodes.description: Internal developer platform, golden paths and shared SDKs for NoSQL data systems teams.

nodes.description: Sourcing SaaS, partners and tooling for NoSQL data systems through enterprise procurement.

nodes.description: Data classification, residency and stewardship for NoSQL data systems.

nodes.description: Discovery, requirements, design and sign-off for new NoSQL data systems features.

nodes.description: Build discipline, branching, code ownership and refactoring practice.

nodes.description: End-to-end test strategy, performance, security and acceptance programs.
